title: NOC 12103 – Conference and Event Planners | GTR Immigration
description: Conference and Event Planners play a key role in corporate functions, tourism, trade shows, and large-scale public events across Canada. Classified under NOC 12103, this occupation benefits from growing post-pandemic demand and offers practical immigration pathways toward Permanent Residence (PR) through skilled worker programs.

classification.teer: 2 – Occupations usually requiring a college diploma or apprenticeship training of two or more years; or supervisory occupations

duties: Promote conference and trade-show services to associations and clients
Coordinate budgets, schedules, and administrative procedures
Arrange accommodation, transport, catering, signage, translation, AV systems, and security
Ensure compliance with municipal and provincial by-laws
Negotiate service contracts and manage vendor payments
Maintain financial records and prepare post-event reports
employment_requirements: A university degree or college diploma in business, tourism, or hospitality administration
Several years of experience in hospitality, tourism, or public relations (may substitute for formal education)
Professional certification in conference or event management may be required
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is projected to create 12,400 new Conference and Event Planner positions, compared with 13,000 new job seekers—making provincial targeting and immigration strategy especially important for international applicants.
additional_information: Conference and Event Planners are increasingly recruited by tourism boards, professional associations, government bodies, and private event agencies.

Strong recovery in hospitality and business-events sectorsStable mid-skill professional careersEligibility for Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs)Employer-supported immigration optionsAnnual earnings typically ranging from $30,800 to $77,000
Why Choose Canada as an Event Planning Professional?
